ai_summary.summary: Failed to fetch Cluster API v1.11.11 manifest from GitHub due to a 504 timeout

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to generate the manifest for 'cluster-api' version 'v1.11.11'. The root cause, as shown in build-log.txt at line 1379, is a 'failed to get component source YAML from URL' error. This error has two components: a 'timed out waiting for the condition' (line 1385) and a more specific 'failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504' (line 1389). A 504 Gateway Timeout indicates a transient network or server-side issue when accessing the GitHub release URL, not a bug in the CAPZ codebase. This is a known transient error class according to the project-specific knowledge.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the test run. Since this is a transient network issue accessing an external GitHub URL, no code changes are required. The test should pass on a subsequent attempt when the GitHub service is responsive.

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to a 504 Gateway Timeout when fetching CAPI release manifests from GitHub

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test suite failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to create a local clusterctl repository. The root cause was a failure to fetch the Cluster API v1.11.11 core components YAML manifest from GitHub (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml), which resulted in a 504 Gateway Timeout error. This is evidenced by the error message in the build log: 'failed to get component source YAML from URL: [timed out waiting for the condition, failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504]' (from build-log.txt line 1379). This type of HTTP 504 error from GitHub is a transient infrastructure issue, not a bug in the CAPZ codebase. The failure occurred during the setup phase before any CAPZ-specific logic was executed.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure was caused by a transient network issue (504 Gateway Timeout) when accessing GitHub during test setup. The suggested fix is to retry the test run, as this is an infrastructure-level transient error.

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to a timeout fetching Cluster API v1.11.11 components from GitHub

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test suite failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to create a clusterctl local repository. The root cause was a failure to fetch the Cluster API v1.11.11 core components YAML from GitHub (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml). The error log from build-log.txt at line 1379 shows: 'failed to get component source YAML from URL: [timed out waiting for the condition, failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504]'. A 504 status code indicates a gateway timeout from GitHub's servers, which is a transient infrastructure issue rather than a bug in the CAPZ codebase. This failure prevented the test suite from initializing the required Cluster API components, causing the entire test run to abort.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure was caused by a transient network issue (504 Gateway Timeout) when fetching external dependencies from GitHub during test setup. The appropriate action is to retry the test run, as this type of infrastructure flake is not indicative of a code defect in CAPZ. No changes to the codebase are required based on this failure.

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to a 504 Gateway Timeout when fetching CAPI release manifests from GitHub

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test suite failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to create a local clusterctl repository. The root cause was a failure to fetch the Cluster API v1.11.11 core components YAML manifest from GitHub (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml), which resulted in a 504 Gateway Timeout error. This is evidenced by the error message in the build log: 'failed to get component source YAML from URL: [timed out waiting for the condition, failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504]' (from build-log.txt line 1379). This type of HTTP 504 error from GitHub is a transient infrastructure issue, not a bug in the CAPZ codebase. The failure occurred during the setup phase before any CAPZ-specific logic was executed.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure was caused by a transient network issue (504 Gateway Timeout) when accessing GitHub during test setup. The suggested fix is to retry the test run, as this is an infrastructure-level transient error.
